@@ -5,6 +5,17 @@ All notable changes to Double Shot Latte will be documented in this file.
 The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
 and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
 
+## [1.1.0] - 2025-11-22
+
+### Changed
+- Improved continuation decision logic with clearer stop conditions
+- Reframed evaluation prompt from "CONTINUE unless..." to "STOP only if..." for better clarity
+- Simplified incomplete work detection language
+
+### Added
+- New stop reason: Detects when a design or plan is being presented to the user for the first time
+- Better differentiation between presenting plans vs. implementing them
+
 ## [1.0.1] - 2024-11-20
 
 ### Fixed

@@ -130,26 +130,3 @@ If a release has issues:
 - Claude Code - Plugin compatibility
 - Bash - Hook script environment
 
-## Common Issues
-
-### Plugin Won't Install
-- Check JSON syntax in all config files
-- Verify paths start with "./" in plugin.json
-- Ensure script is executable (`chmod +x`)
-
-### Hook Not Triggering
-- Verify hook is registered in Claude Code hooks UI
-- Check script permissions
-- Test script execution directly
-
-### Judge Claude Issues
-- Verify `claude` command is available
-- Check recursion prevention environment variable
-- Test structured output parsing
-
-## Maintenance Schedule
-
-- **Monthly**: Test with latest Claude Code version
-- **Quarterly**: Review and update documentation
-- **As needed**: Bug fixes and feature updates
-- **Yearly**: Major version review and cleanup
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -68,14 +68,15 @@ EVALUATION_PROMPT="You are evaluating whether a Claude Code session should conti
 Here is the recent conversation context:
 $RECENT_CONTEXT
 
-CONTINUE (return should_continue: true) unless the assistant has EXPLICITLY indicated one of these clear stop reasons:
+STOP (return should_continue: false) only if the assistant has EXPLICITLY indicated one of these clear stop reasons:
 1. Directly asking for user decisions (\"Which approach would you prefer?\", \"How should I handle...\")
 2. Requesting specific clarification on ambiguous requirements
 3. Work is genuinely complete AND properly documented
 4. Explicitly stated need for user input (\"I need you to...\")
+5. A design or plan is being presented to the user for the first time.
 
-ALWAYS CONTINUE if:
-- Work appears incomplete or partial
+ALWAYS CONTINUE (return should_continue: true) if:
+- Work appears incomplete
 - There are obvious next steps (more files to create, more functions to implement, more tests to write)
 - Assistant mentioned follow-up work (\"Next I'll...\", \"I should also...\")
 - Implementation has TODOs, stubs, or placeholder content
@@ -137,4 +138,4 @@ else
     }'
 fi
 
-exit 0
\ No newline at end of file
+exit 0
